title: Pell Norm PASS: I=-(x²-2y²) identity and the M_B Pell chain from (2,1)
_fixture_purpose: Anchor fixture. Two theorems certified simultaneously. (1) IDENTITY: I=C-F=-(x²-2y²) where x=d-e, y=e. Proof: I=2de-(d²-e²)=2y(x+y)-((x+y)²-y²)=2xy+2y²-x²-2xy=2y²-x²=-(x²-2y²). (2) CHAIN: the M_B Pythagorean tree move M_B(d,e)=(2d+e,d) equals (x,y)→(x+2y,x+y) in Pell variables, which maps P(x,y)=x²-2y² to P(x+2y,x+y)=(x+2y)²-2(x+y)²=-P(x,y). So M_B flips the Pell sign. Starting from (2,1) with P=-1, the M_B chain generates all boundary directions with alternating P=±1.

pell_chain._note: M_B chain from fundamental (2,1); each step flips Pell sign
pell_chain.pattern: P alternates -1/+1/−1/+1; |I|=1 at every step; d/e→1+√2=2.41421...

connections.cert_135: M_B=(2d+e,d) is the Barning-Hall tree move — Pell chain is the M_B spine of the Pythagorean tree
connections.cert_140: I=-(Pell norm); Pell-1→I=+1 (hyp boundary); Pell+1→I=-1 (ell boundary)
connections.cert_138: Plimpton Row 1 (12,5) is a Pell-1 direction: P(7,5)=49-50=-1, I=1
connections.wildberger: arXiv:0806.2490 — Pell equation and QA direction geometry; solutions generate the boundary sequence
